stemHtml: An accurately aligned Inertial Reference System (IRS) will prevent navigation system errors being introduced when planning to fly into the North Atlantic High Level Airspace (NAT HLA). This system should be put into the "Align Mode" in order to achieve optimum performance…

options.a: At cruising level, at least 40 minutes before reaching the boundary of the oceanic airspace.
options.b: As soon as possible - normally when the first crew member enters the flight deck.
options.c: As soon as all the engines of the aircraft have been started.
options.d: After parking brakes have been released.
